title: rubus × boyntonii
intro: Rubus × boyntonii, also known as the Thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant of the Rubus genus, specifically a cross between the American Blackberry (Rubus allegheniensis) and the Thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of anthocyanins and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Rubus × boyntonii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support cancer prevention. Rubus × boyntonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or jam, with herbal preparations often using the plant's leaves, roots, and fruits.
